questions.text: Two spheres of the same size, one of mass 5 kg and the other of mass 50 g are dropped simultaneously from a tower. When they are about to touch the ground, they have the same:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The kinetic energy of an object depends on its mass and velocity. In this case, the spheres have different masses (5 kg and 50 g), so their kinetic energies will not be the same just before touching the ground. The smaller sphere will have a lower kinetic energy due to its smaller mass.

Momentum depends on both mass and velocity. Since the spheres are dropped simultaneously, they will have the same velocity just before touching the ground. However, their masses are different, so their momenta will not be the same. The larger sphere will have a higher momentum due to its greater mass.

When the two spheres are about to touch the ground, they will have the same acceleration due to gravity. The acceleration due to gravity near the surface of the Earth is approximately 9.8 m/s^2. It is the same for all objects regardless of their mass (assuming negligible air resistance). Since both spheres are dropped simultaneously, they experience the same gravitational force acting on them. According to Newton's second law of motion (F = m*a), the force acting on an object is equal to its mass multiplied by its acceleration. As both spheres experience the same gravitational force and have the same acceleration due to gravity, their masses do not affect their speed when they are about to touch the ground. Thus, when they are about to touch the ground, they will have the same velocity or speed.

questions.explanation: When the two spheres are about to touch the ground, they will have the same acceleration due to gravity. The acceleration due to gravity near the surface of the Earth is approximately 9.8 m/s^2. It is the same for all objects regardless of their mass (assuming negligible air resistance). Since both spheres are dropped simultaneously, they experience the same gravitational force acting on them. According to Newton's second law of motion (F = m*a), the force acting on an object is equal to its mass multiplied by its acceleration. As both spheres experience the same gravitational force and have the same acceleration due to gravity, their masses do not affect their speed when they are about to touch the ground. Thus, they will have the same velocity or speed when they are about to touch the ground.

questions.text: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum is responsible for the metabolism of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) plays a role in carbohydrate metabolism, particularly in the synthesis of glycogen and glucose metabolism.
The smooth endoplasmic reticulum is not involved in protein metabolism; this is primarily the function of the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which is studded with ribosomes.
The SER is crucial for lipid metabolism, including the synthesis of phospholipids and cholesterol, which are essential for cell membrane formation.
The smooth endoplasmic reticulum is involved in the metabolism of both carbohydrates (in limited contexts) and lipids, making this option the most comprehensive answer.

questions.explanation: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) primarily functions in the synthesis and metabolism of lipids, making it essential for producing phospholipids and cholesterol used in cellular membranes. Additionally, the SER plays a minor role in carbohydrate metabolism, such as the conversion of glucose and glycogen synthesis.Option A is partially correct as the SER does participate in carbohydrate metabolism, but it is not its primary function. Option B is incorrect because the SER does not metabolize proteins; this function is primarily associated with the rough endoplasmic reticulum. Option C is correct regarding lipid metabolism, but it does not capture the role of carbohydrates, which is why Option D, stating both A and C, is the best answer.
